content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. The only difference between this version and the easy version is that in this version, there is no constraint on the parity of NNN.

This problem is worth 707070 points.**

For an array AAA of length NNN, its range maximum array, denoted BBB, is another array of length NNN constructed as follows.

For each index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N),


Let k=min⁡(i,N−i+1)k = \min(i, N-i+1)k=min(i,N−i+1).
Then, define BiB_iBi​ to be max⁡(Ai−k+1,Ai−k+2,…,Ai+k−2,Ai+k−1)\max(A_{i-k+1}, A_{i-k+2}, \ldots, A_{i+k-2}, A_{i+k-1})max(Ai−k+1​,Ai−k+2​,…,Ai+k−2​,Ai+k−1​).

That is, BiB_iBi​ is the maximum of the length 2k−12k-12k−1 subarray of AAA, centered at index iii.
You are given an array BBB of length NNN.
Count the number of arrays AAA containing positive integers, such that the range maximum array of AAA equals BBB.

The answer can be large, so compute it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of BBB.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the number of arrays AAA whose range maximum array equals BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only valid array is A=[3,4,2]A = [3, 4, 2]A=[3,4,2].
Test case 333: All arrays of the form [2,x,y,2][2, x, y, 2][2,x,y,2] where max⁡(x,y)=5\max(x, y) = 5max(x,y)=5 are valid. There are 999 such arrays.
Test case 555: It can be shown that no valid array exists.
